name: Jumbo Rocks Campground
location: Inside Joshua Tree National Park, high desert
highlights: 124 sites set among massive boulder formations
The most immersive camping experience in the park
bookingWindow: No reservations — first-come, first-served. Arrive early on weekends, especially spring and fall.
seasonalNotes: Open year-round. No water — bring your own. Cold winter nights can drop below freezing.

name: Hidden Valley Campground
location: Inside Joshua Tree National Park, high desert
highlights: 44 sites inside the iconic boulder enclosure
Smaller and more intimate than Jumbo Rocks
bookingWindow: No reservations — first-come, first-served. Arrive before noon on weekends.
seasonalNotes: Open year-round. No water. Adjacent to Hidden Valley Trail and bouldering areas.

name: White Tank Campground
location: Inside Joshua Tree National Park, high desert
highlights: 15 sites — the quietest campground in the park
Adjacent to Arch Rock nature trail
bookingWindow: No reservations — first-come, first-served. Easier to find a site than Jumbo Rocks or Hidden Valley.
seasonalNotes: Open year-round. No water. Small and peaceful.

name: Cottonwood Campground
location: Inside Joshua Tree National Park, south entrance, Colorado Desert
highlights: First-come or reservation depending on season
bookingWindow: Some sites reservable at recreation.gov, others first-come first-served.
seasonalNotes: Open year-round. Water available. Warmer than high desert campgrounds in winter.

name: Skylight Joshua Tree
location: Near the park's north edge, Twentynine Palms
highlights: Intentionally designed desert cabins and domes
Outdoor soaking tubs and fire pits
bookingWindow: Book 2–4 weeks ahead for weekends. Easier midweek.
seasonalNotes: Year-round. Spring and fall most comfortable. Summer requires AC units that some cabins have.

name: Hicksville Trailer Palace
location: Near the park's west entrance, Joshua Tree town
highlights: Vintage trailers on a small ranch — each uniquely themed
Playful, adult, deeply Joshua Tree in spirit
bookingWindow: Book 1–2 months ahead for weekends. Whole-property buyouts available for groups.
seasonalNotes: Year-round. Pool is seasonal. Hot tub year-round. Adults-only vibe.

name: 29 Palms Inn
location: Twentynine Palms, near the north entrance
highlights: Adobe bungalows around a natural pool fed by underground spring
Gardens, resident animals, a genuinely timeless quality
One of the most special small lodges in the American West
bookingWindow: Book 2–4 weeks ahead. Popular with returning guests — some weekends fill months out.
seasonalNotes: Year-round. The garden is especially beautiful in spring. On-site restaurant serves breakfast and dinner.

name: Spin & Margie's Desert Hideaway
highlights: Walking distance to the best coffee and galleries
seasonalNotes: Year-round. Spring is the most charming season with garden in bloom.

name: Sacred Sands
bookingWindow: Only two rooms — book 1–2 months ahead. Sells out for full moons and spring weekends.
seasonalNotes: Year-round. Full moon weekends are especially popular and book far ahead.

name: Pioneertown Motel
highlights: The only place to sleep in Pioneertown itself
Atmospheric bar connected to the motel
bookingWindow: Book 1–2 months ahead for weekends, especially when Pappy's has a big show.
seasonalNotes: Year-round. Basic but atmospheric. Book around Pappy & Harriet's show calendar for maximum experience.

name: Two Bunch Palms
location: Desert Hot Springs, ~45 min from Joshua Tree
highlights: Historic mineral hot springs spa since 1930
Fed by a natural geothermal aquifer
Grotto pools, couples treatments, serious relaxation
Long a Hollywood hideaway — the counterpoint to a strenuous park day
bookingWindow: Book 2–4 weeks ahead. Weekend packages sell out in spring season.
seasonalNotes: Year-round. Summer can be extremely hot — the springs are still a draw but the surrounding heat is intense.

name: L'Horizon Resort & Spa
location: Palm Springs, ~45 min from Joshua Tree
bookingWindow: Book 2–4 weeks ahead. Palm Springs high season is Jan–Apr.
seasonalNotes: Year-round. Peak season Jan–Apr. Summer rates drop significantly but temperatures soar.

name: Parker Palm Springs
location: Palm Springs, ~45 min from Joshua Tree
highlights: The most design-forward major property in the valley
Pool culture, strong restaurant (Norma's), unhurried pace
PSYCH Spa with Gene Autry-era vibes
bookingWindow: Book 1–2 months ahead for peak season. Last-minute summer deals available.
seasonalNotes: Year-round. Peak is Jan–Apr. Norma's for brunch is an institution.

name: Arrive Palm Springs
location: Palm Springs, ~45 min from Joshua Tree
highlights: Smaller, hipper, more affordable entry to the PS hotel scene
Good location on North Palm Canyon Drive
bookingWindow: Book 2–3 weeks ahead for weekends. More accessible than Parker or L'Horizon.
seasonalNotes: Year-round. A great base for a night in PS before or after the park.
